summary.intent: Add a search bar to the UI header that accepts a search term and calls an existing search endpoint.
summary.outcome: A search input was added to the header bar, a /search route was created with a Search.tsx screen that fetches and displays FTS5 results, and TypeScript compilation passed clean.

repo: The app uses electron-vite with hot-reload, so changes are reflected automatically without manual restart.
Search uses SQLite FTS5 under the hood; snippets use «word» syntax for matched terms which get transformed to bold HTML.
Routing is handled in App.tsx with query params (e.g., /search?q=<term>) for passing search state.
The API layer lives in app/src/renderer/src/api.ts and is extended to add new endpoint calls.
Layout.tsx contains the header bar where the search input was pinned to the right side at 220px width.

code.finding: New screen created to display search results; each result shows content-type badge, tool name, session ID prefix, timestamp, and highlighted FTS5 snippet. Clicking a card navigates to the session.
TextInput added to header, navigates to /search?q=<term> on Enter when query is non-empty.
Route /search added pointing to Search.tsx screen.
New API function added to call /api/search endpoint with up to 100 results.

workflow: TypeScript type-checking was run with npx tsc --noEmit after edits to validate correctness before considering the task done.
Multiple sequential edits were made to the same file (Layout.tsx edited 3 times) suggesting iterative refinement during implementation.

friction: Layout.tsx required three separate edit passes, indicating some iteration or correction was needed during implementation.
open_items: Search result highlighting relies on «word» → word transformation; edge cases or XSS safety of snippet rendering may need review.
The result limit is hardcoded at 100; pagination or infinite scroll could be added later.
No error state or empty state handling is mentioned for the Search screen — worth revisiting for UX completeness.
